channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Alan Carr Purchases 19th Century Ayton Castle for New TV Show
item.description: Comedian Alan Carr, recently crowned the winner of The Celebrity Traitors castle, has purchased the 19th Century Ayton Castle in the Scottish Borders. The property boasts 17 bedrooms, 160 acres of lan...

item.title: Mexico Faces Potential Surge in Cartel Violence After Death of Jalisco New Generation Leader
item.description: Mexican authorities are preparing for a possible escalation of violence following the killing of Nemesio 'El Mencho' Oseguera Cervantes, head of the Jalisco New Generation cartel, one of the country's...

item.title: UK approves US use of military bases for defensive strikes on Iranian missile sites
item.description: The UK has granted the United States permission to use British military bases for 'defensive' operations aimed at Iranian missile sites, according to Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer. The decision was ...

item.title: UK Businesses Face Rising Costs Amid New US Tariffs
item.description: UK businesses are facing increased uncertainty and higher costs as new US tariffs are set to take effect. Following a US Supreme Court ruling that struck down the 'Liberation Day' tariffs policy, Pres...

item.title: Queen Camilla Meets French Rape Survivor Gisèle Pelicot at Clarence House, Praises Her Courage in Memoir
item.description: Queen Camilla hosted French rape survivor Gisèle Pelicot for a private 30-minute meeting at Clarence House in London on February 23, 2026. During the encounter, which included tea and an interpreter, ...

item.title: Understanding the Curling Controversy Between Canada and Sweden at the 2026 Winter Olympics
item.description: The 2026 Winter Olympics saw a controversy unfold in a curling match between Canada and Sweden, where tensions escalated due to accusations of cheating. Swedish curler Oskar Eriksson accused Canadian ...

item.title: Top Alternatives to Discord for Users Concerned About Privacy and Age Verification
item.description: As Discord plans to implement age verification by mid-2026, users are expressing concerns over the privacy of submitting government IDs and face scans. The new verification requirement has raised alar...

item.title: Israel Responds to Hezbollah Missile Strike with Airstrikes in Beirut
item.description: Israel launched heavy airstrikes on Hezbollah-controlled areas in southern Beirut on Monday after Hezbollah fired missiles and drones towards Israel in retaliation for the killing of Iranian Supreme L...

item.title: England introduces GP incentives for prescribing Mounjaro to eligible patients
item.description: The UK government will pay GPs in England an annual bonus of £3,000 to encourage the prescription of the weight loss drug Mounjaro, starting in April. Additionally, GPs will receive around £1,000 for ...

item.title: UK Prime Minister launches ethics investigation into minister Josh Simons over Labour Together report controversy
item.description: The UK Prime Minister has instructed his ethics adviser to investigate Cabinet minister Josh Simons following allegations that a think tank he previously ran, Labour Together, commissioned a report sc...

item.title: Residents and Tourists Describe Chaos Following Death of Mexico's Most Wanted Drug Lord
item.description: After the death of Nemesio Oseguera Cervantes, also known as 'El Mencho', the leader of Mexico's Jalisco New Generation (CJNG) cartel, several states in Mexico experienced sudden and widespread violen...

item.title: A Guide to Organizing in the Age of Surveillance: Tools and Tips for Security
item.description: In today's world, organizing grassroots movements comes with a significant risk due to extensive surveillance by the government and big tech companies. Activists and organizers are seeking ways to bui...

item.title: RAF Base in Cyprus Targeted by Suspected Drone Strike; No Casualties Reported
item.description: A suspected drone strike targeted a British RAF base in Cyprus late on Sunday night, according to the Ministry of Defence (MoD). The incident took place at RAF Akrotiri at approximately midnight local...

item.title: New Discovery of Tektite Field in Brazil Linked to Ancient Asteroid Impact
item.description: For the first time ever, scientists have identified a vast field of tektites in Brazil, the glassy material formed when an extraterrestrial object strikes Earth with extreme force. The fragments, name...

item.title: Court Rejects TASC’s Appeal on Sizewell C Sea Defenses and Climate Resilience
item.description: The Appeal Court has rejected Together Against Sizewell C Ltd's (TASC) legal challenge against the government’s decision regarding the Sizewell C nuclear project. TASC had sought to force the governme...

item.title: Astronomers Prepare for New Discoveries in the Kuiper Belt
item.description: Astronomers are on the cusp of major discoveries about the Kuiper Belt, a distant region of our solar system beyond Neptune, known for its icy objects and potential hidden planets. With next-generatio...

item.title: UK Grants US Access to Military Bases for Defensive Strikes on Iran's Missile Sites
item.description: The UK has agreed to allow the US to use British military bases for defensive strikes targeting Iranian missile sites. In response to Iran's increasingly reckless actions, the UK Prime Minister stated...

item.title: Former Skircoat Lodge Care Home Manager and Assistant Found Guilty of Decades of Child Sexual Abuse
item.description: A jury has found that Malcolm Phillips, a 93-year-old former manager of Skircoat Lodge children’s home in Halifax, sexually abused children in his care over a period spanning nearly two decades, from ...

item.title: Peter Mandelson Arrested Over Alleged Misconduct Linked to Jeffrey Epstein Investigation
item.description: Peter Mandelson, the former Labour minister and ex-UK ambassador to the United States, has been arrested on suspicion of misconduct in public office. Officers from the Metropolitan Police detained the...

item.title: Nick Reiner pleads not guilty to first-degree murder charges in deaths of Rob Reiner and Michele Singer Reiner
item.description: Nick Reiner, the 32-year-old son of acclaimed actor and director , has pleaded not guilty to two counts of first-degree murder following the fatal stabbing of his parents, Rob Reiner and Michele Singe...
